initial_program:
% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

row(R)  :- R >= 0, R < N.
col(C)  :- C >= 0, C < M.

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ---------- Copy every nonzero cell unchanged ----------
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V).

% ---------- Vertical propagation ----------
% colour 1 moves downwards (increasing row) until a 2 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,1) :-
    input(R0,C,1), row(R0), col(C),               % source 1
    row(R), col(C), R0 < R,                         % target below source
    N = #count{ X : input(X,C,0), R0 < X, X < R, X != R0, X != R },
    N \ 2 = 0,                                      % no 2 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% colour 2 moves upwards (decreasing row) until a 1 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R0,C,2), row(R0), col(C),               % source 2
    row(R), col(C), R0 > R,                         % target above source
    N = #count{ X : input(X,C,0), R < X, X < R0, X != R0, X != R },
    N \ 1 = 0,                                      % no 1 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% ---------- Horizontal propagation ----------
% colour 1 moves rightwards (increasing column) until a 2 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,1) :-
    input(R,C0,1), row(R), col(C0),               % source 1
    row(R), col(C), C0 < C,                         % target right of source
    N = #count{ X : input(R,X,0), C0 < X, X < C, X != C0, X != C },
    N \ 2 = 0,                                      % no 2 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% colour 2 moves leftwards (decreasing column) until a 1 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C0,2), row(R), col(C0),               % source 2
    row(R), col(C), C0 > C,                         % target left of source
    N = #count{ X : input(R,X,0), C < X, X < C0, X != C0, X != C },
    N \ 1 = 0,                                      % no 1 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% ---------- Exactly one colour per cell ----------
% The choice rule guesses a colour for every output cell.
% The constraints above already force the correct colour for most cells;
% the guess will fill the remaining cells (which stay 0) with colour0.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

row(R) :- R >= 0.
col(C) :- C >= 0.

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ---------- Copy every nonzero cell unchanged ----------
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V).

% ---------- Vertical propagation ----------
% colour 1 moves downwards (increasing row) until a 2 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,1) :-
    input(R0,C,1), row(R0), col(C),               % source 1
    row(R), col(C), R0 < R,                         % target below source
    N = #count{ X : input(X,C,0), R0 < X, X < R, X != R0, X != R },
    N \ 2 = 0,                                      % no 2 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% colour 2 moves upwards (decreasing row) until a 1 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R0,C,2), row(R0), col(C),               % source 2
    row(R), col(C), R0 > R,                         % target above source
    N = #count{ X : input(X,C,0), R < X, X < R0, X != R0, X != R },
    N \ 1 = 0,                                      % no 1 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% ---------- Horizontal propagation ----------
% colour 1 moves rightwards (increasing column) until a 2 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,1) :-
    input(R,C0,1), row(R), col(C0),               % source 1
    row(R), col(C), C0 < C,                         % target right of source
    N = #count{ X : input(R,X,0), C0 < X, X < C, X != C0, X != C },
    N \ 2 = 0,                                      % no 2 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% colour 2 moves leftwards (decreasing column) until a 1 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C0,2), row(R), col(C0),               % source 2
    row(R), col(C), C0 > C,                         % target left of source
    N = #count{ X : input(R,X,0), C < X, X < C0, X != C0, X != C },
    N \ 1 = 0,                                      % no 1 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% ---------- Exactly one colour per cell ----------
% The choice rule guesses a colour for every output cell.
% The constraints above already force the correct colour for most cells;
% the guess will fill the remaining cells (which stay 0) with colour0.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

row(R) :- R >= 0.
col(C) :- C >= 0.

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ---------- Copy every nonzero cell unchanged ----------
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V).

% ---------- Vertical propagation ----------
% colour 1 moves downwards (increasing row) until a 2 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,1) :-
    input(R0,C,1), row(R0), col(C),               % source 1
    row(R), col(C), R0 < R,                         % target below source
    N = #count{ X : input(X,C,0), R0 < X, X < R, X != R0, X != R },
    N \ 2 = 0,                                      % no 2 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% colour 2 moves upwards (decreasing row) until a 1 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R0,C,2), row(R0), col(C),               % source 2
    row(R), col(C), R0 > R,                         % target above source
    N = #count{ X : input(X,C,0), R < X, X < R0, X != R0, X != R },
    N \ 1 = 0,                                      % no 1 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% ---------- Horizontal propagation ----------
% colour 1 moves rightwards (increasing column) until a 2 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,1) :-
    input(R,C0,1), row(R), col(C0),               % source 1
    row(R), col(C), C0 < C,                         % target right of source
    N = #count{ X : input(R,X,0), C0 < X, X < C, X != C0, X != C },
    N \ 2 = 0,                                      % no 2 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% colour 2 moves leftwards (decreasing column) until a 1 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C0,2), row(R), col(C0),               % source 2
    row(R), col(C), C0 > C,                         % target left of source
    N = #count{ X : input(R,X,0), C < X, X < C0, X != C0, X != C },
    N \ 1 = 0,                                      % no 1 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% ---------- Exactly one colour per cell ----------
% The choice rule guesses a colour for every output cell.
% The constraints above already force the correct colour for most cells;
% the guess will fill the remaining cells (which stay 0) with colour0.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

final_program:
% ---------- Grid dimensions ----------
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _) }.

row(R) :- R >= 0.
col(C) :- C >= 0.

output_cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% ---------- Copy every nonzero cell unchanged ----------
output(R,C,V) :- input(R,C,V).

% ---------- Vertical propagation ----------
% colour 1 moves downwards (increasing row) until a 2 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,1) :-
    input(R0,C,1), row(R0), col(C),               % source 1
    row(R), col(C), R0 < R,                         % target below source
    N = #count{ X : input(X,C,0), R0 < X, X < R, X != R0, X != R },
    N \ 2 = 0,                                      % no 2 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% colour 2 moves upwards (decreasing row) until a 1 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R0,C,2), row(R0), col(C),               % source 2
    row(R), col(C), R0 > R,                         % target above source
    N = #count{ X : input(X,C,0), R < X, X < R0, X != R0, X != R },
    N \ 1 = 0,                                      % no 1 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% ---------- Horizontal propagation ----------
% colour 1 moves rightwards (increasing column) until a 2 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,1) :-
    input(R,C0,1), row(R), col(C0),               % source 1
    row(R), col(C), C0 < C,                         % target right of source
    N = #count{ X : input(R,X,0), C0 < X, X < C, X != C0, X != C },
    N \ 2 = 0,                                      % no 2 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% colour 2 moves leftwards (decreasing column) until a 1 or a 5 is met
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C0,2), row(R), col(C0),               % source 2
    row(R), col(C), C0 > C,                         % target left of source
    N = #count{ X : input(R,X,0), C < X, X < C0, X != C0, X != C },
    N \ 1 = 0,                                      % no 1 in between
    N \ 5 = 0.                                      % no 5 in between

% ---------- Exactly one colour per cell ----------
% The choice rule guesses a colour for every output cell.
% The constraints above already force the correct colour for most cells;
% the guess will fill the remaining cells (which stay 0) with colour0.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

#show output/3.
